news.title: Fuji Electric Launches 7th Generation IGBT Module Series with 20% Efficiency Improvement
news.summary: Fuji Electric officially releases 7th generation IGBT modules featuring advanced trench-gate field-stop technology, reducing Vce(sat) by 15-20% and switching losses by 30%
news.author: Fuji Marketing Team
news.content:
March 15, 2024, Tokyo - Fuji Electric today announced the launch of its new 7th generation IGBT module series, marking a major breakthrough in power semiconductor technology. The series features advanced trench-gate field-stop technology with industry-leading performance metrics.

The newly released 7th generation IGBT modules include 2-Pack, 6-Pack, and PIM configurations, with voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V and current ratings from 10A to 1400A. Compared to 6th generation products, the 7th generation IGBT reduces Vce(sat) by 15-20% and switching losses by 30%, improving system efficiency by 2-3 percentage points.

"The 7th generation IGBT technology is the result of years of research and development," said the Director of Fuji Electric's Power Semiconductor Division. "The new products achieve faster switching speeds while maintaining low conduction losses, providing more efficient solutions for industrial drives, renewable energy, and electric vehicle applications."

The new products have passed rigorous reliability testing including power cycling, temperature cycling, and HTRB testing, meeting industrial and automotive grade application requirements. The products are now in mass production, primarily targeting industrial inverters, servo drives, UPS systems, and new energy applications.

news.title: Fuji Electric Expands SiC Production Capacity to Meet EV Market Demand
news.summary: Fuji Electric invests 20 billion yen to expand SiC production lines, planning to triple SiC device capacity with focus on electric vehicle and renewable energy markets
news.author: Fuji Corporate Communications
news.content:
February 28, 2024, Tokyo - Fuji Electric today announced that the company will invest 20 billion yen (approximately $130 million) to expand silicon carbide (SiC) power device production lines to address the rapidly growing electric vehicle and renewable energy markets.

The expansion will take place at Fuji Electric's Matsumoto plant in Japan, with plans to add 6-inch SiC wafer processing capabilities and packaging/testing production lines. The project is expected to be completed in the first half of 2025, at which point Fuji's SiC device capacity will triple. The expansion focuses on 1200V and 650V SiC MOSFETs, primarily targeting electric vehicle onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and photovoltaic inverter applications.

"SiC technology is the core of next-generation power semiconductors," said the President of Fuji Electric. "With the rapid growth of the electric vehicle market, the demand for high-efficiency power devices is becoming increasingly urgent. This expansion will ensure we can meet customer needs and consolidate our leading position in the power semiconductor field."

Fuji Electric plans to increase SiC business revenue share from the current 5% to 15% within three years, and continue to invest in R&D for next-generation trench-gate SiC technology.

news.title: Fuji Electric Strengthens China Market Presence with Power Semiconductor Application Center
news.summary: Fuji Electric establishes Power Semiconductor Application Center in Shenzhen, China, providing local technical support, solution design, and rapid response services
news.author: Fuji Asia Pacific
news.content:
February 10, 2024, Shenzhen - Fuji Electric today announced the official establishment of its Power Semiconductor Application Center in Shenzhen, China, further strengthening local technical support and service capabilities for Chinese customers.

The application center is equipped with advanced testing equipment and an experienced application engineering team, providing customers with the following services: (1) Product selection and solution design support; (2) Reference design and demo board development; (3) On-site technical training and debugging support; (4) Rapid sample and evaluation board supply; (5) Failure analysis and reliability consulting.

"China is one of the world's largest power semiconductor markets," said the President of Fuji Electric Asia Pacific. "The establishment of the Shenzhen Application Center reflects our long-term commitment to the Chinese market. We will work closely with local distributors to provide faster and more professional services to Chinese customers."

The application center will focus on supporting rapidly growing application areas including industrial automation, new energy, electric vehicles, and data centers. Fuji Electric plans to increase power semiconductor sales in China by 50% within two years.

news.title: Fuji Electric Launches New Generation EV Traction Module with 40% Higher Power Density
news.summary: Fuji Electric releases HPnC package IGBT modules for electric vehicle main drive inverters, featuring direct liquid cooling technology and industry-leading power density
news.author: Fuji Automotive Electronics
news.content:
January 20, 2024, Tokyo - Fuji Electric today launched a new generation of electric vehicle traction power modules featuring innovative HPnC (High Power next Core) packaging technology, specifically designed for electric vehicle main drive inverter applications.

The new modules adopt direct liquid cooling technology, reducing thermal resistance by 50% and increasing power density by 40%. The products feature voltage ratings of 750V and 1200V, with continuous current capabilities from 300A to 800A, meeting the main drive requirements for compact electric vehicles and SUVs. The modules include built-in temperature sensors and current detection functions, supporting precise motor control.

"Electric vehicles have increasingly demanding requirements for power density and reliability," said the General Manager of Fuji Electric's Automotive Electronics Division. "Our HPnC modules provide automakers with high-performance, high-reliability solutions through innovative packaging design and advanced cooling technology. This product has been validated by multiple mainstream automakers."

The new modules comply with AEC-Q101 and AQG-324 automotive grade standards, supporting operating temperature ranges from -40°C to 150°C. Fuji Electric plans to cooperate with major global Tier 1 suppliers to promote this product in 2025 mass production vehicles.

news.title: Fuji Electric Showcases Full Range of Power Devices at PCIM Europe 2024
news.summary: Fuji Electric exhibits 7th generation IGBT, SiC MOSFET, IPM and other full series products, focusing on electric vehicle and renewable energy solutions
news.author: Fuji Marketing Team
news.content:
March 11-13, 2024, Nuremberg, Germany - Fuji Electric showcased its full range of power semiconductor products and solutions at PCIM Europe 2024, demonstrating the company's latest technological achievements in the power electronics field.

At this exhibition, Fuji focused on innovative solutions for electric vehicles and renewable energy. In the electric vehicle exhibition area, Fuji demonstrated traction inverter and onboard charger solutions based on 7th generation IGBT and SiC MOSFET, achieving 99% system efficiency; in the renewable energy exhibition area, Fuji demonstrated power module solutions suitable for photovoltaic inverters and energy storage systems.

"PCIM Europe is one of the most important exhibitions in the power electronics industry," said the Sales Director of Fuji Electric Europe. "We hope to demonstrate Fuji's technical strength and complete product portfolio in the power semiconductor field to global customers through this exhibition. During the exhibition, we received over 800 professional visitors and reached cooperation intentions with multiple potential customers."

During the exhibition, Fuji also held technical seminars sharing two topics: "Application of 7th Generation IGBT Technology in Industrial Drives" and "Design Points of SiC MOSFET in Electric Vehicles," which were warmly welcomed by attending engineers.
